name: Quick Duck Rellenos Hunter
recipeIngredient: 2 duck breasts (i.e. breasts of one duck), fat removed (and reserved).
fresh hot peppers to taste -- diced (I used about a dozen Thais)
2 large springs of fresh rosemary, coarsely chopped
salt and freshly ground black pepper.
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt a small amount of duck fat in a very hot frying pan.
2. Season the duck breasts with salt and black pepper.
3. Fry the duck breasts until cooked rare (about 2 minutes per side), then remove and let cool.
4. In the same pan, fry onions until golden. Add more duck fat if needed.
5. While the onions cook, cut the cooled duck breasts into small dice and place in a bowl.
6. When the onions are almost done, add garlic, rosemary, and hot peppers to the bowl. Stir for about a minute to release flavors.
7. Mix everything with the diced duck in another bowl.
8. Stuff the chiles with approximately one-third cheese and two-thirds of the duck mixture.
9. Bake the stuffed chiles at 350°F (175°C) for 10 minutes, or until the cheese melts and they are heated through.
10. Serve as a main course for 2 people, or an appetizer for 6.
[NB. For better results, roast the duck instead of pan-frying it. Coat with garlic and black bean paste, prick the skin thoroughly, then roast at 325°F (160°C) for 1½-2 hours until most fat has cooked off. Cool and shred the meat before using.]
